title: Angel Psysiology as a Psychological Weapon 2 test cases
display_title: Angel Psysiology as a Psychological Weapon 2 test cases
wikitext:
[]: Angel Psysiology as a Psychological Weapon - 2 test cases             Quote Post by Oral Stage » Wed Oct 31, 2012 6:33 am Nearly everyone is already aware of the design elements common to the third angel and the pilots' plugsuits. Each has similar shoulders, thigh gills, ribs etc. My argument is that these serve the express purpose of antagonizing the humans, in particular Shinji, and are not simple pieces of subtext separate from the plot. A definite component of Shinji's terror (and hesitance) at the sight of Sachiel is that he is confronting his traumatic memory of the apparent death of his mother, who was wearing a plugsuit at the time. Indeed, Yui's plugsuit, called a 'divesuit' in the script, arguably has in its little wings a more obvious homologue to Sachiel's exaggerated shoulder blades than the other suits. Another element of Sachiel's design, its stigmata hands, probably further resembles a part of Yui's death that Shinji witnessed, Lillith nailed to the cross in the bowels of the Geofront. Indeed Sachiel's first attack is to break Shinji's right arm, the arm Lillith lost, apparently to necrosis in giving birth to Unit 01. Sachiel's exposed core is a further taunt in this direction, destabilizing Shinji by lending salience to an already pervasive unconscious conflict. Obviously, Shinji is not the only target of this emotional abuse. Commanders Ikari and Fuyutski were both present at the contact experiment and Ritsuko was informed of it by her mother. It's less obvious what effect this had on any of them, although Commander Ikari does give an ironic smirk when he announces that he's sending Unit 01 out to attack it. For further proof that Shinji is so terrified of plugsuits, remember that Shinji screamed is lungs out when Unit 01's helmet slid off, revealing that its natural forehead is the face and shoulders of Sachiel. For want of a better word, it seems that Sachiel 'evolved' to taunt Shinji. How is this demonstrated? Consider Iruel, which attacks Ritsuko's mother, the Magi, right after Ritsuko reflects to herself, 'Today, as ever, my mother is just fine, but... am I only aging?'. It would seem that no angel fails to frighten on some ironic level. Did it appear out of the ether because these fears manifested in Ritsuko's mind? Or was it laying dormant in B-wing until it sensed weakness? Psychological weapons are employed by every angel, demonstrating intimate knowledge of the characters, but the angels also predate the vulnerabilities they exploit. Who do we know who can time travel?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Oct 31, 2012 7:10 am IMO you're reading far too much into this, and assuming the people who designed the plugsuits had some idea as to how the Third Angel would appear -- somewhat unlikely given that Yui's CE occurred ten years before Sachiel first appeared.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Oct 31, 2012 9:35 am Of course, we don't know what esoteric information from the Secret Dead Sea Scrolls was available that might have influenced the design.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Oct 31, 2012 12:26 pm The SDSS continue to make absolutely no sense unless you assume it's an early working draft of the episode scripts that somehow fell into the show itself.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Oct 31, 2012 2:05 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Of course, we don't know what esoteric information from the Secret Dead Sea Scrolls was available that might have influenced the design.

[]:             Quote Post by Oral Stage » Wed Oct 31, 2012 6:48 pm Do the plugsuits look like Sachiel or does Sachiel look like the plugsuit? Remember that the plugsuits predate Sachiel. I also don't think the kaiju-attack-type of Angels really cares about or really percieves individual humans, even the more advanced ones had trouble understanding basic human thought processes without breaking their minds.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Nov 01, 2012 11:59 am Do the plugsuits look like Sachiel or does Sachiel look like the plugsuit? Remember that the plugsuits predate Sachiel.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Nov 01, 2012 12:17 pm NemZ wrote: The SDSS continue to make absolutely no sense unless you assume it's an early working draft of the episode scripts that somehow fell into the show itself.

[]:             Quote Post by Oral Stage » Fri Nov 02, 2012 5:12 am Seele seems to know, or at least admit to knowing, how many angels will attack, when an angel will invade the Geofront and when angels will start taking evas into themselves. The first one might be derived from 2I data. The second was wrong but it was, according to Gendou, depicted in the Dead Sea Scrolls. The third was another miss. Zeruel got eaten by the Eva, not the other way around. There is no reason, as I see it, to think that any part of a pilot's plugsuit needs to resemble Sachiel in order to work. How could dressing up like Sachiel increase sync rates in any way? The simpler explanation is that Sachiel developed a form that antagonized its enemies, intuiting the enemy's psychology in a similar way it did his location

[]:             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Sat Nov 03, 2012 8:28 pm Oral Stage wrote: Seele seems to know, or at least admit to knowing, how many angels will attack, when an angel will invade the Geofront and when angels will start taking evas into themselves. The first one might be derived from 2I data. The second was wrong but it was, according to Gendou, depicted in the Dead Sea Scrolls. The third was another miss. Zeruel got eaten by the Eva, not the other way around.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Nov 04, 2012 7:49 pm NemZ wrote: The SDSS continue to make absolutely no sense unless you assume it's an early working draft of the episode scripts that somehow fell into the show itself.

[]:             Quote Post by Oral Stage » Mon Nov 05, 2012 1:57 am Tribblepoo wrote: I also remember someone in SEELE mentioning a "prophesied form" for one of the angels, but I might be mistaken. It's a quibbling point anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Nov 05, 2012 4:37 pm The time travel explanation might make sense if Rei went back and just watched but she gives Shinji advice, endangering her own existence.

[]:             Quote Post by Oral Stage » Mon Nov 05, 2012 5:27 pm Maybe Kaworu is going back in time too and fucking shit up 24/7. I'm not a physicist, is there any reason something can be immune to time travel paradoxes?

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Mon Nov 05, 2012 8:28 pm Oral Stage wrote: That's an interesting point. There are Angels that speak to the humans. Leliel, Arimasael and Kaworu. Leliel seems to offer some quite definite ideas about human psychology saying (although it could just be Shinji talking to himself) that the self is made of the self observed and the one observing that. Arimasael is ignorant that the pain it feels is lonelyness. And Kaworu... Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Nov 06, 2012 3:04 am Oral Stage wrote: Maybe Kaworu is going back in time too and fucking shit up 24/7. I'm not a physicist, is there any reason something can be immune to time travel paradoxes?

[]:             Quote Post by Oral Stage » Sun Nov 11, 2012 11:28 am Very interesting. I've got some reading to do over the summer OT: can we at least agree that there is equal basis in believing that Sachiel got its physiology from the plugsuits and in believing that nerv got it's plugsuit design from Sachiel. We have no well supported mechanism for either scenario, nor any exposition on the subject. This is assuming of course that we're ignoring the subtext-only explanation

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Nov 11, 2012 4:08 pm The problem is we know that plug suits have existed atleast since Yui disappeared. Sachiel is days old, at most.

[]:             Quote Post by Oral Stage » Sun Nov 11, 2012 9:30 pm Barring any quantum/time travel effects, I guess. I'll try write a proper argument against it being solely an aesthetic design choice in a few days. (Exams and shit)
